questions.specification:
The 'cradle-to-cradle' (C2C) design framework categorizes materials into two distinct 'nutrient' types to ensure they can be returned to productive use. Which row correctly identifies the intended end-of-life path for these material categories?

| | Biological Nutrients | Technical Nutrients |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| A | Incineration for energy recovery | Disposal in managed landfills |
| B | Return to the natural biosphere | Circulation in industrial cycles |
| C | Processing via open-loop recycling | Breakdown via anaerobic digestion |
| D | Storage in specialized facilities | Conversion into organic compost |

A company that produces heavy-duty aircraft engines implements the following workflow for engines that have reached their flight-hour limit:

| Phase | Action |
| :--- | :--- |
| I | The engine is completely broken down into its smallest individual parts. |
| II | Every part is chemical-cleaned and inspected using high-precision sensors. |
| III | Degraded parts are substituted with upgraded components that meet current standards. |
| IV | The engine is reassembled and certified to perform with a full new-product warranty. |

Identify the strategy for waste mitigation presented in this workflow.

A manufacturing team is analyzing three different end-of-life pathways for their consumer electronics line. The table below describes the specific processes applied to each product category.

| Product Category | End-of-Life Process Description |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Category 1** | The product is completely disassembled, and all components are inspected; it is then rebuilt using new and original parts to meet the same quality and performance standards as a new product. |
| **Category 2** | The product is cleaned and serviced to return it to a good, functional working order, though it may not meet its original factory performance specifications. |
| **Category 3** | The product is broken down so that its constituent materials can be processed and used as raw material for the manufacturing of new, different products. |

Which row correctly identifies the waste mitigation strategies for these three categories?

options.markscheme: Category 1 is remanufacturing because the product is stripped down and rebuilt to original 'as-new' specifications. Category 2 is reconditioning because it is returned to 'good working order' without necessarily meeting original specs. Category 3 is recycling because the materials are processed back into a raw state for a new production cycle.

questions.specification:
The table below describes two different approaches to managing products and materials at the end of a specific life stage.

| Strategy | Action taken |
| :--- | :--- |
| **X** | A technician replaces a broken charging port on a smartphone so the device can be used again by its owner. |
| **Y** | Used aluminium beverage cans are collected, melted down, and the recovered metal is used to manufacture new automotive parts. |

Which strategies do X and Y represent?

A company that produces aluminium beverage cans reduces the thickness of the can walls by 0.05 mm while maintaining the required pressure resistance. This change significantly decreases the total volume of raw material required for the same production output.

Which waste mitigation strategy is best described by this design improvement?

An electronics company redesigns a smartphone to have a thinner external casing and fewer internal components, while transitioning the user manual to a digital-only format.

Which waste mitigation method is being applied in this redesign?

Commercial manufacturers are increasingly adopting waste mitigation strategies to comply with environmental regulations and improve sustainability. A typical example is the high-volume modular office photocopier shown in **Figure 1**.

**Figure 1: A modular commercial photocopier**

![photocopier](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/ramen-ramen-ib-economics-63c6735e-c260-47b7-8534-9c74e74b0973-1771937587718/e6513426-48a3-4747-b49b-2bf978734724.jpeg)

parts.content: Outline how the shift from physical media distribution to digital cloud services (such as providing software manuals online) is an example of dematerialization.
parts.markscheme:
- Dematerialization involves reducing the total material and energy throughput of a product or service <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Moving manuals from paper to digital format eliminates the need for physical materials (paper, ink, packaging) and the energy required for their production/transport <marks>1 mark</marks>

<note>Award 1 mark for the definition/explanation of dematerialization and 1 mark for the specific application to the digital shift.</note>

parts.content: Discuss whether the implementation of a manufacturer "take-back" scheme for used photocopiers is an example of **product recovery** or **pollution reduction**.
parts.markscheme:
- It is product recovery because the manufacturer collects the end-of-life product to extract value by salvaging functional parts or materials for reuse <marks>1 mark</marks>
- It is pollution reduction because it ensures hazardous components (like toner chemicals or lead-soldered boards) are disposed of through controlled channels rather than landfill <marks>1 mark</marks>
- It is generally considered both, as recovering materials automatically reduces the environmental pollution associated with virgin material extraction and waste disposal <marks>1 mark</marks>

<note>
Maximum 1 mark for each point:
- Identifying product recovery aspect
- Identifying pollution reduction aspect
- Providing a concluding/integrating statement
</note>

parts.content:
A circular economy aims to keep resources in use for as long as possible.

Explain how waste can be minimized through **reconditioning**, **re-engineering** and **recycling** strategies in the management of commercial photocopiers.
parts.markscheme:
### Reconditioning <marks>3 marks max</marks>
- Reconditioning involves returning a used product to its original "as-new" functional and aesthetic state <marks>1 mark</marks>
- For a photocopier, this involves cleaning, repairing, and replacing worn internal rollers or belts while keeping the original chassis <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Minimizes waste by extending the primary life of the machine, delaying the need for a new replacement <marks>1 mark</marks>

### Re-engineering <marks>3 marks max</marks>
- Re-engineering involves redesigning a product or its components to improve performance or update its functionality <marks>1 mark</marks>
- For a photocopier, this could involve replacing an obsolete analog processing unit with modern digital circuit boards while retaining the mechanical frame <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Minimizes waste by preventing the disposal of large mechanical structures that are still functional, even if the technology inside is outdated <marks>1 mark</marks>

### Recycling <marks>3 marks max</marks>
- Recycling is the process of breaking down a product into its base materials to be reprocessed into new products <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Components like the metal frame or plastic panels are shredded and melted down to create raw material for different products <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Minimizes waste by diverting materials from landfill and reducing the demand for virgin raw material extraction <marks>1 mark</marks>

<note>
- Do not award marks across clusters (e.g., mixing reconditioning and re-engineering points).
- Mark as [3] + [3] + [3].
</note>

questions.specification:
A global beverage company redesigns its packaging to use a new polymer that allows for a 40% reduction in total material weight per bottle without compromising structural integrity.

Which waste mitigation strategy is best illustrated by this design change?

The graph below illustrates a historical trend in the production of aluminium beverage containers.

![Material Usage Graph](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/ramen-ramen-ib-physics-new-a6b2eb9b-085f-4ea2-bf82-dfeaab3e73f7-1771929956947/933ecc4f-f8a3-42b4-a695-1f62109ae440.jpeg)

Which waste mitigation strategy is primarily responsible for the reduction in material mass shown in the graph?

A structural engineer replaces a solid aluminium bracket with a 3D-printed version that uses a complex internal geometry. This new component provides the same mechanical performance while requiring significantly less raw material. Which waste mitigation strategy is most accurately described by this change?

The following table lists shifts in consumer product formats over several decades:

| Product | 1990s Format | 2020s Format |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Music | Compact Disc (CD) | Digital streaming |
| Reference | Multi-volume encyclopedia | Online database |
| Photography | 35mm film roll | Internal storage sensor |

Which waste mitigation strategy is demonstrated by these transitions?

A heavy equipment manufacturer collects used hydraulic pumps from its customers. These pumps are fully stripped down, every component is inspected against original engineering drawings, and any parts that do not meet current tolerances are replaced. The finished product is then tested and sold with the same warranty and performance guarantees as a brand-new unit.

Which waste mitigation strategy is described in this scenario?
